title: Inventory satellite slope-stability, rainfall-trigger, landslide inventory, and population-exposure data sources for the Himalayan region
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite slope-stability products (InSAR), rainfall-trigger models (GPM, CHIRPS), landslide inventory databases, and population-exposure data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit Himalayan terrain validation assessment.
inputs: Sentinel-1 InSAR documentation and mountainous-terrain validation studies
NASA Global Landslide Hazard Assessment documentation
GPM and CHIRPS precipitation product documentation for mountainous terrain
Global Landslide Database and national landslide inventories
Population and settlement data for Himalayan valleys
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, resolution, and documented limitations — particularly InSAR performance in steep/forested terrain and rainfall accuracy in mountainous regions.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, Himalayan terrain validation, and data-gap documentation.

title: Map landslide susceptibility using satellite deformation and rainfall-trigger data for at least two Himalayan countries
outcome: A sub-national landslide-susceptibility analysis for at least two Himalayan countries, documenting InSAR deformation detection, rainfall-trigger thresholds, and explicit terrain-specific limitations for steep, forested, cloud-covered terrain.
inputs: Sentinel-1 InSAR data for target Himalayan regions
GPM or CHIRPS rainfall data for target regions
Published landslide inventory data for validation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with susceptibility methodology and terrain-specific limitations.
validation_method: Replicator confirms InSAR data extraction, rainfall-threshold methodology, and terrain-specific limitation documentation for each target country.
done_condition: Susceptibility analysis includes methodology, terrain-specific limitations, InSAR performance documentation, rainfall-threshold methodology, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Estimate population exposure to landslide susceptibility in Himalayan valleys with critical infrastructure documentation
outcome: A population-exposure analysis combining susceptibility maps with settlement locations, road networks, and critical infrastructure in Himalayan valleys, with explicit documentation that susceptibility maps are probabilistic, not predictive.
inputs: Road network and critical infrastructure location data
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with exposure methodology and susceptibility-vs-prediction distinction.
validation_method: Replicator confirms exposure methodology, settlement data integration, and susceptibility-vs-prediction documentation.
done_condition: Exposure analysis includes methodology, population estimates, infrastructure documentation, susceptibility-vs-prediction distinction, and replication status.

title: Red-team susceptibility-map overstatement and false-prediction risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how susceptibility maps could be mistaken for predictions, how InSAR limitations could create false confidence in deformation monitoring, and how results could be used for inappropriate village-relocation or road-closure decisions.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every susceptibility claim has probabilistic-language documentation, InSAR limitations, and explicit non-prediction disclaimers. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every susceptibility claim is either blocked, softened to probabilistic language with explicit terrain limitations, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match disaster-management and community-preparedness decision needs in Himalayan districts
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the susceptibility-exposure analysis would change any real decision for national disaster-management agencies or community-based preparedness programs in Himalayan districts without creating perverse incentives.
inputs: disaster-management agency context for target countries
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for disaster-management and community-based audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that susceptibility maps are treated as predictions, or that InSAR limitations create false confidence in monitoring capability. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Nepal National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Authority officer), named decision (e.g., district-level early warning pilot expansion), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for village-relocation decisions, not for road-closure without ground verification), and replicated field-reality review.
